title: Repeat Protocol
story: [Meridian-7 Log #010] rEx's encoding includes a repetition protocol: certain signals consist of the same character repeated 3-5 times. Is this a compression format, or a form of emotional expression? Either way, you need to extract these repeating patterns precisely from the data. This is rEx's final test for you.
promptChallenge: Write a prompt to have AI generate a regex that matches strings composed of the same letter repeated 3-5 times
hints: [Hint] You need to match the repetition of the "same" letter
[Hint] Backreferences can match previously captured content
[Hint] The quantifier {n,m} can specify repetition count
promptHints: Tell the AI you want to match repetition of the same letter, not just any letters
Hint the AI to use backreferences to ensure letter consistency
Clarify the repetition range is 3 to 5

feedback.onPass: [Test Passed] Repetition protocol cracked. All Chapter Two signals parsed.
feedback.onFail: [Parse Failed] Repetition pattern recognition failed. Same-letter repetition differs from random letters.
feedback.onPerfect: [Test Passed] You've mastered all of rEx's encoding rules. It seems... satisfied.
feedback.onDirectWrite: [Manual Override] Manual protocol breach. Chapter Two, an elegant close.
